assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: Two epic files exist: epic-1-trustify-backend.md and epic-2-trustify-ui.md. The impact-map.md documents 'Strategy: by-repository' and lists Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend) and Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui) with tasks correctly assigned to each.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md Epic Grouping table documents summaries as 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' and 'TC-9006: trustify-ui', matching the required format. All 7 task files reference these exact summaries in their 'Parent Epic' sections (e.g., task-1: 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-backend', task-4: 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-ui').

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks have a '## Parent Epic' section. Tasks 1-3 (Repository: trustify-backend) have 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-backend'. Tasks 4-7 (Repository: trustify-ui) have 'Parent Epic: TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Repository-to-epic mapping is correct in all cases.

assertion_results.text: Incorporates links are created from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks)
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of 'Incorporates' links anywhere in the outputs. The impact-map.md documents Epic Grouping and Inherited Fields but does not reference the Incorporates link type or any link creation from the Feature to the Epics.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks (1, 2, 3) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (4, 5, 6, 7). Dependencies are correctly ordered: Task 1 (no deps) -> Task 2 (depends on 1) -> Task 3 (depends on 2). Task 4 (depends on Task 2, cross-epic) -> Task 5 (depends on 4) -> Task 6 (depends on 5) -> Task 7 (depends on 6). All dependency references point to lower-numbered tasks.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain: ## Repository (single repo name), ## Target Branch (main), ## Description, at least one of ## Files to Modify or ## Files to Create, ## Implementation Notes, ## Acceptance Criteria, ## Test Requirements. Verified each file individually.

assertion_results.text: Epics are created with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key
assertion_results.evidence: The epic-*.md files contain only paragraph descriptions without structured fields. Neither epic-1-trustify-backend.md nor epic-2-trustify-ui.md contains an explicit issue type field set to 'Epic' or a parent field set to the feature issue key (TC-9006). The impact-map.md labels them as 'Epic 1' and 'Epic 2' in a table but does not explicitly specify the issue type name or parent field configuration.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 tasks have '## Repository' with a single value: tasks 1-3 have 'trustify-backend', tasks 4-7 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain ## Target Branch (all set to 'main'), ## Description (substantive multi-sentence descriptions), ## Acceptance Criteria (checkbox lists), and ## Test Requirements (checkbox lists). Verified in every task file.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files end with 'Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex>' lines. Each contains exactly 64 lowercase hex characters: task-1 (21c1635947...a7b47c), task-2 (4461feaf63...207bc7), task-3 (49328fa73c...77e931), task-4 (95ce3bb99f...fe445c), task-5 (9763b5d3f7...55592d), task-6 (52d506249b...701b6c), task-7 (6e781ebc27...c29756b). All are unique, non-placeholder values using sha256-md: prefix.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention rationales across all 7 tasks follow the prescribed format 'Applies: task modifies/creates <specific-file> matching the convention's <scope>.' Examples: task-1 has 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/remediation/model/summary.rs matching the convention's module structure scope', task-4 has 'Applies: task modifies src/api/models.ts matching the convention's API layer scope'. No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Inherited Fields' section documents: 'Priority: Major (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)'. This confirms the Feature's priority (Major) was propagated to all created tasks and epics.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Inherited Fields' section documents: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)'. The project CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so the default scope ('both') applies, meaning fixVersions should be propagated to tasks. The Feature has fixVersions (RHTPA 1.5.0) and it was propagated.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md contains an '## Inherited Fields' section documenting: 'Priority: Major (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)'. Both inherited field values and their propagation status are clearly documented.
